name: King Cake
recipeIngredient: Dough of your choice for a large coffee cake (approximate proportions):
2 oz Fresh compressed yeast or 4 package Active dry yeast
1 Dried bean or tiny (3/4" long) china baby doll
5 Or more small bottles of assorted food coloring
recipeInstructions:
1. After mixing the dough and adding the bean or doll (if desired), let it rise.
2. Shape the dough into an oval ring with approximate dimensions: about 2-1/2" thick and 3" high at the highest point.
3. Sprinkle the top of the cake evenly with sugar.
4. Make swirls with food coloring all over the top, with each color slightly overlapping the next.
5. Embed pieces of candied fruit in the top of the cake, concentrating most of them in a band about 1-1/4" wide around the entire cake.
6. Set the cake on a baking sheet and bake according to instructions from your recipe.
7. The finished cake should be lightly browned wherever the dough shows through; the parts covered with colored sugar should appear slightly crusty.
The purchased cakes do not have fruit embedded in them.
Shape: an oval ring about 2-1/2" thick and 3" high at the highest point, with decoration as elaborate and colorful as possible to resemble a jeweled crown. The bean or doll can be baked into the batter or pushed into the finished cake from underneath, hidden so no one knows in advance which slice will designate the monarch for the week.
